tips: Use npm audit to find and fix security vulnerabilities in dependencies
The npx command runs packages without installing them globally
Use npm outdated to see which dependencies have newer versions
Semantic versioning: ~1.2.3 means patch only, ^1.2.3 means minor and patch
Use package-lock.json to lock dependency versions for reproducible builds
Run npm ci in CI for faster, deterministic installs from lockfile
Use npm init -y to quickly scaffold a new package.json
The scripts section in package.json automates common tasks
Use npm link to test a local package in another project
Prefer npm install --save-exact for pinning dependency versions
Use overrides in package.json to force transitive dependency versions
npm pack creates a tarball to inspect what will be published
